Title: Heterologous Production of Forskolin in Tobacco (Nicotiana tabacum) via Glandular Trichome Specific Engineering and Metabolic Flux Redirection

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates cAMP signaling pathway; used for treatment of heart complications, respiratory disorders, high blood pressure, obesity and asthma
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of endogenous CBTS genes increases GGPP availability and strongly boosts heterologous forskolin accumulation by redirecting precursor flux away from CBT-diol biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; GC-MS confirmation of CBT-diol loss; LC-MS/MS measurement of GGPP/FPP/GPP; stable transformation with forskolin pathway; metabolite profiling; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtabCBTSs1/2/3 encode endogenous tobacco functions required for CBT-diol biosynthesis in glandular trichomes. 2. Simultaneous editing generated a cbts mutant lacking CBT-diols. 3. The cbts mutant showed ~4.8-fold higher GGPP in GTs with little transcriptomic disturbance. 4. Blocking CBTS-mediated endogenous diterpenoid metabolism substantially increased forskolin production from both constitutive and GT-specific engineered pathways.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse growth under 16 h light/8 h dark at 25°C
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly associated with glandular trichomes/aerial tissues where CBT-diols accumulate; CBTS promoter is GT-specific
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: NtabCBTSs1/2/3; orthologous references NM_001325450.1; NsylCBTS2a HM241151.1; NsylCBTS2b HM241152.1; NsylCBTS3 HM241153.1
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and glandular trichomes of transgenic tobacco
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing CBTS genes creates a chassis with reduced endogenous diterpenoid competition and improved GGPP supply for production of heterologous high-value terpenoids such as forskolin
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant alterations in morphology or glandular trichome density; only minor transcriptomic changes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The edited targets were S-subgenome CBTS copies; NtabCBTSt was inferred dysfunctional and not targeted. Effect on forskolin is indirect through precursor redirection rather than direct catalysis of forskolin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates cAMP signaling pathway; used for treatment of heart complications, respiratory disorders, high blood pressure, obesity and asthma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as part of the heterologous pathway that enables forskolin production in N. benthamiana and transgenic tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable multigene transformation in tobacco; LC-MS/MS detection of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfTPS2 was one of six C. forskohlii genes sufficient in combination for forskolin biosynthesis in plants. 2. Inclusion of CfTPS2 in stable multigene constructs supported detectable forskolin production in tobacco. 3. Yield increased further when expression was GT-specific and precursor competition was removed by cbts editing.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in cyclization/formation of the labdane diterpene scaffold in forskolin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms diterpene scaffold precursor for target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Driven transgenically in tobacco under 35S or CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GT-specific expression and cbts background increase forskolin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core scaffold-forming enzyme for reconstructing forskolin biosynthesis in plant chassis; useful in stacked diterpenoid engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper did not dissect the individual contribution of CfTPS2 apart from pathway reconstitution.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates cAMP signaling pathway; used for treatment of heart complications, respiratory disorders, high blood pressure, obesity and asthma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required as part of the heterologous pathway that enables forskolin production in N. benthamiana and transgenic tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable multigene transformation in tobacco; LC-MS/MS detection of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfTPS3 was one of six genes co-expressed to reconstitute forskolin biosynthesis in plants. 2. Its expression in tobacco contributed to stable forskolin production. 3. Production increased strongly under GT-specific expression and in cbts mutant background.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in conversion of GGPP-derived precursor into downstream labdane diterpene intermediate in forskolin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Labdane diterpene precursor generated from GGPP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for downstream oxidation and acetylation to forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Driven transgenically in tobacco under 35S or CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GT-specific expression and cbts background increase forskolin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential pathway component for heterologous forskolin production in plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Individual enzymatic step not characterized in this paper; role inferred from prior pathway studies cited and successful pathway reconstruction.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of labdane diterpenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates cAMP signaling pathway; used for treatment of heart complications, respiratory disorders, high blood pressure, obesity and asthma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required oxidative enzyme within the heterologous forskolin pathway enabling forskolin formation in plant hosts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable multigene transformation in tobacco; LC-MS/MS detection of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH15 was included in the six-gene module sufficient for plant production of forskolin. 2. Stable expression supported detectable forskolin in tobacco. 3. Higher yields were obtained with GT-specific expression and precursor-flux redirection.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes pathway oxidation/hydroxylation step(s) on labdane diterpene intermediate toward forskolin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms oxidized intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Driven transgenically in tobacco under 35S or CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Endogenous tobacco CPRs support activity; GT-specific expression improves production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary oxidative tailoring enzyme for pathway reconstruction in plant chassis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes plant endogenous CPRs likely provide electron transfer support for P450 activity.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of labdane diterpenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates cAMP signaling pathway; used for treatment of heart complications, respiratory disorders, high blood pressure, obesity and asthma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required oxidative enzyme within the heterologous forskolin pathway enabling forskolin formation in plant hosts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable multigene transformation in tobacco; LC-MS/MS detection of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH11 was one of the six genes sufficient in combination for forskolin biosynthesis in plants. 2. Inclusion in stable tobacco constructs enabled forskolin production. 3. Yields improved with GT-targeting and cbts mutation.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes pathway oxidation/hydroxylation step(s) on labdane diterpene intermediate toward forskolin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms oxidized intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Driven transgenically in tobacco under 35S or CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Endogenous tobacco CPRs support activity; GT-specific expression improves production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary P450 component for engineering complete forskolin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific oxidation order was not resolved in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative tailoring steps of labdane diterpenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates cAMP signaling pathway; used for treatment of heart complications, respiratory disorders, high blood pressure, obesity and asthma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required oxidative enzyme within the heterologous forskolin pathway enabling forskolin formation in plant hosts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable multigene transformation in tobacco; LC-MS/MS detection of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP76AH16 was part of the six-gene module sufficient for forskolin production in plants. 2. It contributed to low but detectable forskolin accumulation under constitutive expression. 3. Greater accumulation occurred with GT-specific expression and CBTS knockout background.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes pathway oxidation/hydroxylation step(s) on labdane diterpene intermediate toward forskolin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms oxidized intermediate leading to target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Driven transgenically in tobacco under 35S or CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Endogenous tobacco CPRs support activity; GT-specific expression improves production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary oxidative tailoring enzyme for complete forskolin pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific oxidation position/order not specified in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal acylation step of labdane diterpenoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates cAMP signaling pathway; used for treatment of heart complications, respiratory disorders, high blood pressure, obesity and asthma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Terminal pathway enzyme required in the heterologous six-gene module for forskolin biosynthesis in plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in N. benthamiana; stable multigene transformation in tobacco; LC-MS/MS detection of forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfACT1-8 was one of the six C. forskohlii enzymes sufficient in combination to produce forskolin in plants. 2. Stable expression in tobacco supported formation of the final acetylated product. 3. GT-specific expression and precursor redirection markedly enhanced final yield.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to a hydroxylated forskolin precursor to form forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Driven transgenically in tobacco under 35S or CBTS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GT-specific expression and cbts background increase final product accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal enzyme for final forskolin production in engineered plants
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper identifies CfACT1-8 as the acetyltransferase in the pathway but does not provide accession or subcellular localization.
